title: elaphrium inaguense
intro:
Elaphrium inaguense is a type of plant species, scientifically known as Elaphrium, but it is actually a synonym for the plant species 'Machaerophyllum inaguense' or 'Machaerophyllum pumilum'. However, I believe you may be referring to a different plant as there is limited information available about the 'Elaphrium inaguense' plant. 

However, 'Machaerophyllum pumilum' has been found in the wild and has been used traditionally for medicinal purposes. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, and its leaves have been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, due to their potential medicinal properties.

As for the health benefits of 'Machaerophyllum pumilum', they are not well-documented, but some potential benefits include soothing digestive issues and acting as an anti-inflammatory. 

The herbal preparations of 'Machaerophyllum pumilum' are not well-documented and the information available is limited.
